chartPattern.explanation: The price action shows significant volatility and a general downtrend from late 2025 through early 2026, followed by a sharp upward move in May 2026. No clear classic chart pattern is discernible within this data.

fundamentals.name: Snowflake Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Snowflake Inc. provides a cloud-based data platform for various organizations in the United States and internationally. The company's platform includes artificial intelligence (AI) Data Cloud, which enables customers to consolidate data into a single source of truth to drive meaningful business insights, build data applications, and share data and data products, as well as applies AI for solving business problems. It serves financial services, advertising, media and entertainment, retail and consumer goods, healthcare and life sciences, manufacturing, technology, telecom, travel and hospitality, and government and defense industries, as well as the public sector. the company has a collaboration with OpenAI, L.L.C. for the development of AI solutions for joint enterprise customers that deliver tangible return on investment. The company was formerly known as Snowflake Computing, Inc. and changed its name to Snowflake Inc. in April 2019. Snowflake Inc. was incorporated in 2012 and is based in Menlo Park, California.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and free cash flow, but currently unprofitable on an operating basis and trading at a high valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-26.
positives: Revenue growth 33.5% YoY, with analyst estimates for the current fiscal year at 53.9%.
Positive free cash flow of $1.74B TTM.
Gross margin of 67.1% indicates strong pricing power on services.
Last quarter's EPS beat estimates by 21.9%.
Analyst consensus is 'strong buy' with a mean target of $281.31.
negatives: Operating margin of -22.2% and profit margin of -23.8% indicate unprofitability.
High forward P/E of 107.25 and Price/Sales of 19.29 suggest a very high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 142.91 indicates significant leverage.
Return on equity of -54.9% and return on assets of -9.7% show poor capital efficiency.

news.paragraph: Snowflake's stock is experiencing a post-quarter rally, fueled by positive commentary from prominent analysts like Jim Cramer and Dan Ives, who highlight the company's understanding of the AI transition and its Cortex Code driving increased consumption. Oppenheimer raised its price target to $295, citing AI strategy wins and increased consumption. While the company's last reported quarter beat EPS estimates, its operating and profit margins remain negative, though free cash flow is positive.

headlines.title: Jim Cramer Expects Snowflake’s Post-Quarter Rally to “Roll On”
Jim Cramer Notes “Snowflake Understands the Transition to AI”
Oppenheimer Says Cortex Code Is Driving Increased Consumption; Raises PT On Snowflake (SNOW) To $295
Is SNOW Stock A Buy? Dan Ives Sees 10% Upside In Snowflake's Price Following AI Strategy Win
Should You Buy Snowflake Stock After Its Recent Surge? The Answer Might Surprise You.
